extraction.one_line_summary: HERRADA Jesus won from 26-rider breakaway while SEPCUSS defended red jersey against EVENEPOEL's probe attack in final 200m
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step allowed 26-rider break with THOMAS (best GC at start) gaining 6min, revealing comfort with time bonuses outside Jumbo control.
EVENEPOEL tested SEPCUSS only final 200m on category-1 finish, suggesting unwillingness to commit before Friday's Tourmalet showdown.
CARTHY attacked when lower-GC riders bridged, exploiting Quick-Step's selective chase logic to protect his own ranking.
Break took 1h20min to establish despite only 90km stage, indicating peloton's aggressive early tempo management on summit finish day.

rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: best-placed GC rider in 26-man break at 6min finishing only 5th shows abysmal Vuelta form continues
rider_signals.SEPCUSS Kuss: positive: matched EVENEPOEL acceleration after time trial confirms climbing legs intact despite previous day's TT effort
rider_signals.AYUSO Juan: neutral: crashed in neutral zone but finished with peloton, avoided time loss but injury concern lingers
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: neutral: only probed final 200m on cat-1 finish reveals strategic patience or unwillingness to show cards before Tourmalet
rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: positive: opportunistically attacked when Quick-Step allowed lower-tier riders to bridge, showing GC defense awareness
